input: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced a new partnership with Samsung at the CES conference in Las Vegas.
President Biden met with French President Macron at the White House to discuss NATO defense spending.
Google DeepMind researchers published a breakthrough paper in Nature about protein folding.
Maria Garcia, a software engineer at Microsoft in Seattle, won the ACM Distinguished Paper Award.
The Bank of England raised interest rates while the European Central Bank held steady in Frankfurt.
Dr. James Wilson from Stanford University presented his findings at the IEEE conference in Tokyo.
Amazon opened a new fulfillment center in Nashville, creating 5000 jobs in Davidson County.
Lionel Messi scored twice as Inter Miami defeated New York Red Bulls at Chase Stadium in Fort Lauderdale.
Tesla shares dropped after CEO Elon Musk announced layoffs at the Fremont factory in California.
The World Health Organization warned about rising malaria cases in sub-Saharan Africa during its Geneva summit.
Jennifer Lopez and Ben Affleck were spotted at a restaurant in Beverly Hills last Saturday evening.
The United Nations Security Council voted on sanctions against North Korea at the headquarters in New York.
Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang unveiled the new H200 chip at the GTC conference in San Jose.
Oxford University researchers collaborated with Pfizer to develop a new treatment for Alzheimer disease.
The Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell testified before Congress about inflation in Washington DC.
SpaceX launched a Falcon 9 rocket from Cape Canaveral carrying Starlink satellites into orbit.
Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel received the Presidential Medal of Freedom at the White House.
Toyota announced plans to build a new electric vehicle factory in Greensboro, North Carolina.
Professor Sarah Chen at MIT published groundbreaking research on quantum computing in Science magazine.
The European Parliament passed new AI regulations proposed by Commissioner Thierry Breton in Brussels.
Sundar Pichai confirmed Google will invest 2 billion dollars in a new data center in Malaysia.
The Metropolitan Museum of Art in New York unveiled a new exhibition featuring works by Picasso and Monet.
JPMorgan Chase CEO Jamie Dimon warned about geopolitical risks at the Davos World Economic Forum in Switzerland.
Taylor Swift performed at Wembley Stadium in London as part of her record-breaking Eras Tour.
The Indian Space Research Organisation successfully launched the Chandrayaan-4 mission from Sriharikota.
Mark Zuckerberg announced that Meta would hire 10000 engineers for its AI division in Menlo Park.
The Supreme Court heard arguments from lawyers representing both the State of Texas and the ACLU.
Cristiano Ronaldo joined Al Nassr in Riyadh after leaving Manchester United at the end of the season.
Researchers at Johns Hopkins University in Baltimore discovered a new biomarker for early cancer detection.
The Tokyo Stock Exchange suspended trading after a system glitch affected the Nikkei 225 index.
Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau addressed the General Assembly at the United Nations in New York.
Samsung Electronics reported record profits driven by strong demand for memory chips in South Korea.
Serena Williams received a lifetime achievement award at the ESPY ceremony in Los Angeles.
The International Monetary Fund downgraded its growth forecast for Latin America citing persistent inflation.
Chef Gordon Ramsay opened his newest restaurant in the Bellagio hotel in Las Vegas.
The European Space Agency collaborated with NASA on the Artemis III mission to the lunar south pole.
Warren Buffett sold a large stake in Bank of America according to filings with the SEC.
The University of Cambridge partnered with AstraZeneca to develop new treatments at their lab in Oxford.
Vice President Kamala Harris visited a manufacturing plant in Detroit operated by General Motors.
Anthropic co-founder Dario Amodei discussed AI safety at a panel at the Aspen Ideas Festival in Colorado.
The Reserve Bank of Australia kept rates unchanged while economists at Goldman Sachs predicted further tightening.
LeBron James signed a new contract extension with the Los Angeles Lakers worth 100 million dollars.
The African Union summit in Addis Ababa discussed the ongoing conflict in Sudan with representatives from Egypt and Ethiopia.
Satya Nadella confirmed that Microsoft will integrate OpenAI technology into all Office 365 products.
Hurricane Maria caused severe flooding in Puerto Rico and the Dominican Republic last September.
The Louvre Museum in Paris announced a special exhibition of Leonardo da Vinci drawings from the Royal Collection.
ByteDance CEO Liang Rubo testified before the Senate Commerce Committee about TikTok data practices in Washington.
The Nobel Prize in Physics was awarded to Professor Alain Aspect of Universite Paris-Saclay in Stockholm.
TSMC broke ground on a new semiconductor factory in Phoenix, Arizona with support from the Department of Commerce.
Pope Francis addressed thousands of pilgrims in St. Peter Square at the Vatican during his weekly audience.
